document.title: Risk Management Report for G Series

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for H&abyz G Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models under EN ISO 14971:2019-based risk management activities.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports technical documentation by documenting risk analysis, risk evaluation, risk control, residual risk acceptability, and production/post-production information review for the listed Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models.
keyPoints: The product is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector with wired or wireless models G1417CW, G1717CW, G1417FW, G1717FW, G1417CWP, G1717CWP, G1417FWP, and G1717FWP.
Applicable national and regional regulations listed are Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
The device classification is stated as IIa and expected service life is approximately 5 years.
The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children.
Before risk control, 2 risks were acceptable and 47 were unacceptable; after risk control, 49 were acceptable and 0 were unacceptable.
Overall residual risks are determined acceptable through warning, operating method, adverse event reporting, and usability expert review.

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: If the plan changes during the life cycle of the medical device, a record of the changes shall be maintained in the risk management file.

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4; Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

concepts.name: Residual risk evaluation
concepts.evidence: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in an acceptable region.

concepts.name: Benefit-risk analysis
concepts.evidence: The benefit-risk analysis is used to determine if the residual risk is outweighed by the expected benefits of the intended use of the medical device.

concepts.name: Reasonably foreseeable misuse
concepts.evidence: Might be misused by maintenance and calibration error of operator.

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance.

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is a digital imaging solution for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children.

evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Purpose

evidence.claim: The detector is intended to replace film-based radiographic diagnostic systems and support precise case diagnosis and treatment planning in real time.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis and treatment planning on a real time basis for physicians and radiologists.

evidence.claim: Applicable national and regional regulations listed are EU MDR 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017; Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

evidence.claim: Risk analysis references the Risk Management FMEA Table, document number A-TCF-10-105.
evidence.evidenceText: Please refer to Risk Management FMEA Table (Doc. No. A-TCF-10-105).

evidence.claim: Risk acceptability criteria define acceptable risk levels as 1 to 4 and unacceptable risk levels as 5 to 25.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

evidence.claim: Before risk control, the report identifies 2 acceptable-region risks and 47 unacceptable-region risks.
The narrative analysis says 43 risks were identified, while the table total is 49.
evidence.evidenceText: Through the identification of known and foreseeable hazards, 43 risks were identified and estimated. The risk evaluation results are determined that; - Acceptable region: 2 ea - Unacceptable region: 47ea

evidence.claim: After risk control, all 49 risks are in the acceptable region and none are in the unacceptable region.
Overall residual risks are determined acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Finally, the overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through the warning, operating method and adverse event reporting.

evidence.claim: Production and post-production information should be obtained via A-TCF-10-161 Post-Market Surveillance.
evidence.evidenceText: Refer to A-TCF-10-161 Post-Market Surveillance for getting the information of production and post- production phases.

evidence.claim: The Quality manager performs risk analysis for collected production and post-production information once every 12 months.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk analysis for the collected information (once every 12 months)

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text lists European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and Republic of Korea Medical Device Act; FDA authority is not supported by the source text.
Risk count evidence is conflicting: section 6.3 states 43 risks were identified, while risk evaluation tables show a total of 49 risks.
Several OCR/text quality issues are present, including misspellings and malformed references such as EN 15322-1 where the standards list appears to include EN 15223-1.
The source path filename includes 250205, but the document text provides Rev. Date Oct 18, 2024; no separate document date of Feb 5, 2025 is stated in the source text.
